title: AgentSpawnSidecar
description: Hook-readable spawn authorization record. Written by the Orchestrator before calling the Agent tool. Validated by the PreToolUse hook before any agent spawn is authorized. This is the enforcement artifact — not a human-readable task description.

taskId.description: ULID. Must match the AgentTaskManifest taskId for this spawn.

session_id.description: Claude Code runtime session ID, or the sentinel value 'session-id-unavailable' when the runtime session ID cannot be retrieved. When sentinel is present, the hook falls back to mtime freshness as the primary gate.

runtime_subagent_type.description: Always 'general-purpose'. This is the Claude Code API value for tool_input.subagent_type. It is not the agent's role — it is the runtime harness type. Do not use this field to identify which agent is spawning.

agent_role.description: The framework role of the agent being spawned. Validated by the hook against the allowed roster. This is the field the hook uses for identity — not runtime_subagent_type.

riskLevel.description: Risk classification from the pre-spawn protocol. Drives HITL gate requirement.

riskClass.description: Domain-specific risk class. Examples: frontend-ui, server-api, database-schema, auth-flow, payment-flow. Free string — adopters define their own taxonomy.

domains.description: Abstract domain labels for this task. Examples: ['frontend', 'checkout']. Distinct from interfacesTouched in the AgentTaskManifest which carries concrete file paths.

hitlApproved.description: Whether human-in-the-loop approval has been obtained. Must be true for riskLevel 'high' or 'critical'. The hook blocks spawn if false on HIGH/CRITICAL risk.

issuedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this sidecar was written. Used for TTL validation — the hook rejects manifests older than the configured TTL (default 30 minutes). Combined with mtime check for replay protection.

promptHash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the full spawn prompt (tool_input.prompt). Computed by the Orchestrator before writing the sidecar. Validated by the hook to detect prompt tampering between sidecar issuance and spawn.

tool_use_id.description: Claude Code tool_use_id from the Agent tool call. Null at pre-spawn time; populated by PostToolUse hook for correlation. Optional — present in stricter implementations.
